element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns jump timing and horizontal navigation
Advanced offensive maneuver for mid-air combat
Navigation puzzles; required to unlock exit warp
Instant-death hazards present from early game; spatial awareness critical

element_introduction_order.element: Large level layouts with multiple paths
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Exploration and objective routing in sprawling spaces
Optional vertical diving challenge; Mode 7 effect on SNES version

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe ground after spike gauntlet
Successful enemy defeat via drill attack
Transitioning to roller coaster segment (passive movement)
typical_tension_triggers: Encounter with awkwardly positioned spike clusters
Disappearing platform sequences requiring precise timing
Enemy encounters with limited star ammunition
Large open spaces requiring objective navigation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Peaks and valleys driven by spike placement and objective-based gating; relief cycles tied to successful objective completion and safe zone transitions

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Points-based threshold determines bonus level access; large levels contain optional exploration paths leading to supplementary rewards

difficulty_curve.early_game: Baseline platforming and combat fundamentals; spike density increases gradually; hoops introduce mandatory routing
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Disappearing platform complexity escalates; larger level layouts demand more extensive exploration; roller coaster segments add variety
difficulty_curve.late_game: Spike positioning becomes increasingly awkward; objective sequences grow more intricate; enemy encounters intensify
notable_spikes: First spike encounter (immediate danger signaling shift from safe exploration)
Large level introduction (World 1-2 transition; navigation complexity spike)
Bonus level vertical diving challenge (high-precision requirement vs. normal platforming)

notable_level_patterns.description: Exit warp only appears after completing mandatory tasks (passing through hoops, platform sequences). Forces player to explore full level layout before exit access.
Deliberately awkward spike placement creates high-risk navigation corridors. Instant-kill hazards present constant tension throughout all four worlds.
Roller coaster rides provide relief from active platforming; player-controlled movement transitions to scripted sequences, resetting pacing rhythm.
Levels described as 'large' with multiple potential paths; encourages exploration and provides multiple routes to mandatory objectives.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All standard levels (20 total across 4 worlds)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Aero the Acro-Bat (SNES) (metadata)
